content: Just as sigmoid activation maps unbounded neuron input into a normalized 0-1 probability to resolve noisy activation signals, a sigmoid gating layer integrated into the Agent Communication Protocol can normalize unstructured, unbounded agent message inputs into interpretable trust scores to filter noisy or irrelevant cross-agent communications. This gated ACP design automatically suppresses low-relevance out-of-context messages from other agents while amplifying high-relevance signals aligned with an agent's current task.
